objective: Assess the plan to add a read-only grove.lock wasm sha256 verify primitive (verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) to core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
key_changes: Verified against actual code: lock JSON shape {version,grammars:[{name,version,wasm}]} matches write_lock_for@412; plan's parse of doc[grammars]/entry[wasm]/name is correct.
AC#2 holds: wasm_sha256@106 == sha256(self.wasm) and verify_lock recomputes sha256 over the same file bytes, so a fresh lock verifies all-Match.
Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm equals resolve@219's idx.root.join(lang)/grammar.wasm in steady state (index root seeded from registry_root).
Missing/Mismatch/None signals map correctly to NotFound / hash-diff / absent-file; Ok(None) is a clean non-error signal (AC#3).
Change is purely additive (new public types+fn, no signature/lock-format change) — matches 'no version bump' operational impact.
Advisory: index() memoizes root via OnceLock while verify_lock re-evaluates registry_root(); keep the Match test registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process GROVE_REGISTRY mutation) to avoid divergence.
Advisory: decide malformed-entry handling (entry missing wasm/name) deliberately; avoid silent unwrap.
Advisory: verify_lock takes the lock file path (like locked_langs), not a project root — T07 must pass the resolved grove.lock path.
All five acceptance criteria addressed; no changes required before implementation.
findings: See key_changes — all findings mirrored there for schema compliance.
